title: corytholoma douglassii var. verticillata
intro: Corytholoma douglassii var. verticillata, also known as Douglas' Corytholoma or Douglas' Corydalis, is a perennial plant that belongs to the borage family (Boraginaceae). The plant is said to have medicinal properties due to the presence of alkaloids and other bioactive compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Corytholoma douglassii var. verticillata include its ability to relieve pain, reduce inflammation, and act as an antispasmodic, making it a potential treatment for conditions such as arthritis, muscle spasms, and menstrual cramps. Herbal preparations of the plant are often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used externally or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
